key_insight: Optimized thermal spray processing, especially HVOF, can produce dense, strongly adherent wear-resistant coatings with markedly improved hardness and wear performance.
engineering_relevance: These coatings can extend the service life of critical industrial components exposed to abrasive and erosive environments.
method: Deposited ceramic, metallic alloy, and composite powders using thermal spray methods.
Compared plasma spraying and HVOF under controlled process conditions.
Evaluated microstructure, adhesion strength, density, hardness, and wear resistance.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports systematic experimental evaluation showing that coating performance strongly depends on deposition technique and process parameters, with HVOF-sprayed composite coatings performing best.
watch_item: Results are comparative and process-dependent, so performance gains may not transfer directly without matching substrate, powder, and spray settings.
paper_title: Design and Fabrication of Wear-Resistant Coatings Using Thermal Spray Techniques
